mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.4pp, or signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during low inflation periods signals robust supply-side growth that boosts equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in >55% of low-inflation expansion months, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the federal funds rate above 0.5% triggers immediate repricing of duration risk and liquidity constraints, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, or signal fails to beat buy-and-hold in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and stable unemployment indicates labor market tightening that compresses wage growth expectations, supporting corporate margins and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.15) in panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle signals reduced household leverage and lower construction sector demand, dragging on equity valuations and producing negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Housing declines during tightening periods generate positive returns in >60% of cases, or signal shows no predictive power in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects stable manufacturing output that reduces supply chain uncertainty and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of positive INDPRO months.
